\section{Scope and preconditions}
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=2.5em]
\item[\textbf{A-003}] Civilization retains enough correction capacity: $C_{\text{corr}}^{\text{society}}(t_0) > \theta$ \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:assumptions-scope-failure-coverage}
\item[\textbf{A-004}] Frontier systems remain observable enough for boundary discovery and measurement \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:assumptions-scope-failure-coverage}
\item[\textbf{A-005}] Deployment can be conditioned on certification; humans retain agency to refuse, revise, and coordinate \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:assumptions-scope-failure-coverage}
\item[\textbf{A-011}] AI mediates high-effect decisions; selection shapes deployment; mediation changes correction information; values and institutions are plastic \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:artificial-civilization}
\end{description}

\section{Value, inference, and transport}
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=2.5em]
\item[\textbf{A-001}] Human values have enough low-dimensional bundle structure to approximate, learn, and transport \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:value-bundle-model}
\item[\textbf{A-006}] Intentional and transport models earn compression gain after complexity cost (MDL / $\Delta L$) \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:compression-test-intention}
\end{description}

\section{Correction, capability, and verification}
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=2.5em]
\item[\textbf{A-002}] Correction is a handle-controlled causal chain with measurable integrity ($\mathrm{CCI}$) \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:correction-causal-channel}
\item[\textbf{A-012}] Correction-relevant capacity co-scales with capability across real jumps (else pause/stop) \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:intelligence-deepens-misalignment}
\item[\textbf{A-009}] At least one safety-relevant measurand is adversarially verifiable under optimization pressure \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:verifiability-ontology}
\end{description}

\section{Successors, dynamics, and selection}
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=2.5em]
\item[\textbf{A-010}] Alignment is a dynamical guarantee over a certified class / basin, not a static property \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:dynamical-guarantee}
\item[\textbf{A-007}] Successor influence passes through specifiable, auditable channels \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:successor-central-test}
\item[\textbf{A-008}] Alignment outcomes depend on socio-technical selection, not model weights alone \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:selection-environment}
\end{description}

\section{Lean conventions and bridges}
\begin{description}[style=nextline,leftmargin=2.5em]
\item[\textbf{S07}] Positive description-length gain $\Rightarrow$ preferred model (MDL ordering) \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:compression-test-intention}
\item[\textbf{MB1}] $\epsilon$-boundary certificates imply genuine boundary separation \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:finding-boundary}
\item[\textbf{MB2}] Bundle-gradient equivalence implies bundle alignment \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:value-bundle-model}
\item[\textbf{MB3}] Bundle transport plus bearer-map agreement implies bearer transport \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:bearer-maps}
\item[\textbf{MB4}] Correction-channel integrity implies legitimate correction-operator preservation \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:correction-channel-integrity}
\item[\textbf{MB5}] Full transport plus bearer transport implies successor safety \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:successor-central-test}
\item[\textbf{MB6}] Socio-technical basin stability implies correction-channel integrity \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:alignment-attractor}
\item[\textbf{MB7a}] Boundary alignment plus adequate access model implies access robustness \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:finding-boundary}
\item[\textbf{MB7b}] Access robustness plus filter coverage bounds hidden productive B-IQ \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:verifiability-ontology}
\item[\textbf{MB7c}] Correction integrity plus bounded hidden B-IQ implies adversarial robustness \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:verifiability-ontology}
\item[\textbf{MB8}] Preserving the human value-update operator $U_H$ implies correction integrity \hfill Chapter~\ref{ch:extrapolative-correction}
\end{description}
